title: rubus chaerophyllus subsp. rohlenae
intro: Rubus chaerophyllus subsp. rohlenae, also known as Rohlena's bramble, is a subspecies of the Rubus chaerophyllus species, a type of wild raspberry native to the Czech Republic. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus chaerophyllus subsp. rohlenae include its potential to help reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant's roots and rhizomes to make a decoction.
